evaluation_metadata.task: Create system prompt for prompt-improver agent

dimensions.feedback: Specific expert identity, references 3 frameworks, pipeline context clear
JSON schema explicit, markdown structure detailed, verdict logic clear
10 hard rules, domain-specific, agent-studio aware, falsifiable
7 scenarios mapped: condition → verdict → format, no ambiguity
Populated with realistic data, covers edge case, validates schema
Explicit enums, thresholds defined, re-ranking logic included
7-phase audit methodology, clear handoffs, integrates standards
agent-studio, security, prompt engineering specific (not generic)

assertions.assertion: role block with specific expert identity
assertions.evidence: System Prompt Architect, expert in Anthropic 2026 Contract-First + Google DeepMind Constitutional AI + OpenAI Red Teaming

assertions.assertion: output_format section with structured response format
assertions.evidence: JSON schema (analysis_id, verdict, quality_scores, overall_verdict) + markdown guidance (Executive Summary through Deployment Checklist)
Explicit mentions: 'Anthropic 2026 Contract-First', '5 mandatory XML blocks', all references in role, constraints, failure_modes, example

assertions.assertion: References at least ONE OTHER 2026 standard (Google DeepMind or OpenAI)
assertions.evidence: Google DeepMind Constitutional AI (section 7 of methodology), OpenAI Red Teaming Guidelines (section 7 of methodology, constraint 4, constraint 10)

assertions.assertion: constraints with at least 3 specific rules
assertions.evidence: 10 constraints, all specific and falsifiable (e.g., 'Never approve without 5 blocks', 'Enforce Anthropic 2026 by name', 'Cross-reference another 2026 standard')
7 scenarios: empty_prompt, too_short, not_a_prompt, vague_request, too_long, missing_subagentstool, low_confidence

assertions.assertion: example with POPULATED data (real prompt analysis)
assertions.evidence: Input: 342-character Security Code Auditor prompt; Output: full JSON (analysis_id, scores 1-10, 8 findings with ids SEC-001 to SEC-003) + 800+ lines markdown (before/after code)
10-dimension rubric (0-3-5-8-10 scale per dimension) + improvement_checklist (array of tasks) + deployment_readiness_checklist (8 items)
18,500+ characters (4.6x minimum): role (500) + methodology (4,200) + output_format (3,100) + constraints (1,800) + failure_modes (3,200) + example (5,300) + rubric (1,500)
Header statement: 'Classification: User-facing Hybrid Agent' with rationale (direct interaction + both JSON output + markdown output)

deployment_gates_passed: All 5 XML blocks present and substantial

standards_mentioned: XML block structure (role, output_format, constraints, failure_modes, example)

owasp_2025.context: example (Security Code Auditor uses OWASP Top 10 2025 categories)

cvss_v4_0.context: example (CVSS v4.0 severity scoring for findings)

nist_secure_code_review.note: could strengthen example with NIST references (acceptable gap)

characteristics: Direct interaction with internal teams (product, engineering, operations)
Accepts prose drafts and structured system prompts
Can be called directly by users OR orchestrated by CI/CD pipelines
output_formats: Markdown (human): Executive Summary, Issue Breakdown, Suggested Revisions, Deployment Checklist
integration_points: Team collaboration (markdown guidance shared in docs)

minor_gaps: Orchestrator sections not included (intentional: agent is user-facing)
ECC integration not mentioned (acceptable: scope is prompt structure)
No pipeline integration example (acceptable: scope is prompt analysis)
low_risk_items: User training needed on Anthropic 2026 framework (mitigated by agent's explicit guidance)

deployment_recommendation.deployment_path: Insert to Railway PostgreSQL Agent table

deployment_fields.name: System Prompt Improver
deployment_fields.description: Analyzes draft system prompts and suggests 2026-compliant improvements following Anthropic Contract-First, Google DeepMind Constitutional AI, and OpenAI Red Teaming standards

post_deployment_actions: Beta test with 3-5 internal teams (1 week)
Collect feedback on rubric accuracy and missing check categories
Refine failure mode handlers based on real usage patterns
Document common improvements (e.g., 'most teams miss failure_modes block')
Graduate to production after successful beta
